     The Numbers Business Report

X-Men: Apocalypse (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $155,442,489Details
International Box Office $387,095,057
Worldwide Box Office $542,537,546
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $11,943,827
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $22,006,822
Total $33,950,649
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Since the dawn of civilization, he was worshipped as a god. Apocalypse, the first and most powerful mutant from Marvel’s X-Men universe, amassed the powers of many other mutants, becoming immortal and invincible. Upon awakening after thousands of years, he is disillusioned with the world as he finds it and recruits a team of powerful mutants, including a disheartened Magneto, to cleanse mankind and create a new world order, over which he will reign. As the fate of the Earth hangs in the balance, Raven with the help of Professor X must lead a team of young X-Men to stop their greatest nemesis and save mankind from complete destruction.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$65,769,562 (42.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.36 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:28.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$178,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,150 opening theaters/4,153 max. theaters, 4.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $214,923,951

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 27th, 2016 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: May 18th, 2016 (Wide) (France)
May 19th, 2016 (Wide), released as X-Men: Apocalisse (Italy)
May 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Mexico)
May 19th, 2016 (Wide) (South Korea)
May 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Netherlands)
Video Release: September 9th, 2016 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of violence, action and destruction, brief strong language and some suggestive images.
(Rating bulletin 2423 (Cert #50091), 5/4/2016)
Running Time: 136 minutes
Franchise: X-Men
Cast, crew, or production detail: Visual Effects
Film format: 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Mutants
Social setting: End of the World
Source material: Marvel Comics
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: Ancient Egypt
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Twentieth Century Fox, Marvel Studios, TSG Entertainment, Bad Hat Harry Productions, Kinberg Genre, Hutch Parker, Donners’ Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Since the dawn of civilization, he was worshipped as a god. Apocalypse, the first and most powerful mutant from Marvel’s X-Men universe, amassed the powers of many other mutants, becoming immortal and invincible. Upon awakening after thousands of years, he is disillusioned with the world as he finds it and recruits a team of powerful mutants, including a disheartened Magneto, to cleanse mankind and create a new world order, over which he will reign. As the fate of the Earth hangs in the balance, Raven with the help of Professor X must lead a team of young X-Men to stop their greatest nemesis and save mankind from complete destruction.

Opening Weekend:$65,769,562 (42.3% of total gross)

Theater counts:4,150 opening theaters/4,153 max. theaters, 4.2 weeks average run per theater
